questions.testName: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The function $f$ defined by $f(t)=14t+9$ gives the estimated length, in inches, of a pothos plant t months after Shruti purchased it. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 9 in this context?
questions.questionText: The function $f$ defined by $f(t)=14t+9$ gives the estimated length, in inches, of a pothos plant t months after Shruti purchased it. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 9 in this context?

choices.text: Shruti will keep the pothos plant for 9 months.
The pothos plant is expected to grow 9 inches each month.
The pothos plant is expected to grow to a maximum length of 9 inches.
The estimated length of the pothos plant was 9 inches when Shruti purchased it.

questions.testName: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: At a convention center, there are a total of 375 visitors. Each visitor is located in either room A, room B, or room C. If one of these visitors is selected at random, the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room A is $0.48$, and the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room B is $0.24$. How many visitors are located in room C?

questions.testName: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: Quinidra drove her car on a straight road to a certain location. The graph models Quinidra's distance, in miles, from her home as a function of time, in hours. Which of the following could the model describe?
questions.questionText: Quinidra drove her car on a straight road to a certain location. The graph models Quinidra's distance, in miles, from her home as a function of time, in hours. Which of the following could the model describe?

choices.text: Quinidra drove away from her home at a constant speed for 1 hour, spent 4 hours at the location, then continued to drive away from her home at a constant speed for 1 hour.
Quinidra drove away from her home at a constant speed for 1 hour, spent 4 hours at the location, then drove back to her home at a constant speed for 1 hour.
Quinidra drove away from her home at an increasing speed for 1 hour, spent 4 hours at the location, then drove back to her home at a decreasing speed for 1 hour.
Quinidra drove away from her home at an increasing speed for 1 hour, drove at a constant speed for 4 hours, then drove back to her home at a decreasing speed for 1 hour.

questions.prompt: In triangle $FGH$ and triangle $KLM$, the measures of angles $G$ and $L$ are each 30°. The lengths of $\overline{GH}$ and $\overline{LM}$ are each 34 centimeters, and $\frac{GH}{GF}=\frac{LM}{LK}$. Which additional piece of information would be necessary to prove that triangle $FGH$ is congruent to triangle $KLM$?
questions.questionText: In triangle $FGH$ and triangle $KLM$, the measures of angles $G$ and $L$ are each 30°. The lengths of $\overline{GH}$ and $\overline{LM}$ are each 34 centimeters, and $\frac{GH}{GF}=\frac{LM}{LK}$. choices.text: The lengths of $\overline{FG}$ and $\overline{KM}$ are each 17 centimeters.
The measures of angles $H$ and $M$ are each 60°.
The measures of angles $F$ and $K$ are each 90°.

questions.prompt: A beaker containing a liquid is placed on a table. questions.prompt: Quinidra drove her car on a straight road to a certain location. The graph models Quinidra's distance, in miles, from her home as a function of time, in hours. Which of the following could the model describe?
questions.questionText: Quinidra drove her car on a straight road to a certain location. The graph models Quinidra's distance, in miles, from her home as a function of time, in hours. Which of the following could the model describe?

choices.text: Quinidra drove away from her home at a constant speed for 1 hour, spent 4 hours at the location, then continued to drive away from her home at a constant speed for 1 hour.
Quinidra drove away from her home at a constant speed for 1 hour, spent 4 hours at the location, then drove back to her home at a constant speed for 1 hour.
Quinidra drove away from her home at an increasing speed for 1 hour, spent 4 hours at the location, then drove back to her home at a decreasing speed for 1 hour.
Quinidra drove away from her home at an increasing speed for 1 hour, drove at a constant speed for 4 hours, then drove back to her home at a decreasing speed for 1 hour.
